--- title: "Hello, Python" subtitle: "An example Quarto post with executable Python code." date: "2026-05-15" categories: [python, example] format: html: code-fold: false jupyter: python3 --- This post is a `.qmd` file with **Python code chunks**. Quarto runs the code at render time and inlines the output (numbers, tables, plots) into the page. To start a new post, copy this folder and edit `index.qmd`. ## A tiny dataset ::: {#3fccd742 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd df = pd.DataFrame({ "month": pd.date_range("2026-01-01", periods=6, freq="MS"), "revenue": [120, 138, 155, 162, 180, 205], "channel": ["organic"] * 6, }) df ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
month revenue channel
0 2026-01-01 120 organic
1 2026-02-01 138 organic
2 2026-03-01 155 organic
3 2026-04-01 162 organic
4 2026-05-01 180 organic
5 2026-06-01 205 organic
``` ::: ::: ## A quick plot ::: {#b4d55b39 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import matplotlib.pyplot as plt fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3.5)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["revenue"], marker="o") ax.set_title("Monthly revenue, organic channel") ax.set_ylabel("Revenue ($k)") ax.grid(alpha=0.3) fig.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/cell-3-output-1.png){width=564 height=326} ::: ::: ## Notes - Use `jupyter: python3` in the YAML header to enable Python execution. - Heavy outputs (model training, big queries) get cached on first render via `freeze: auto` in [_quarto.yml](../../_quarto.yml) — only the chunks you change get re-executed.
